assertion.title: cosrule(x,y)=sqrt(1+x²-2x*cos(y)) and sinpi(x)=sin(πx) compound operators reduce total nodes 303→297
assertion.statement: Adding cosrule binary operator (normalized cosine law) and sinpi unary operator compresses eq37 Cosine Rule from 11→7 nodes and eq45 Standing Wave from 10→8 nodes, reducing total nodes from 303 to 297 while maintaining 50/50 exact matches.

details.hypothesis: Compound operators cosrule(x,y)=sqrt(1+x²-2x*cos(y)) and sinpi(x)=sin(πx) can replace multi-node subexpressions in Cosine Rule and Standing Wave equations.
changes: Added cosrule binary operator: cosrule(x,y) = sqrt(1 + x² - 2x*cos(y)) — normalized law of cosines
Added sinpi unary operator: sinpi(x) = sin(πx) — absorbs π constant into operator
Updated eq37 permutation template: hypot(a-b*cos(θ), b*sin(θ)) → b*cosrule(a/b, θ) (11→7 nodes)
Updated eq45 permutation template: A*sin(π*n*x/L) → A*sinpi(n*x/L) (10→8 nodes)
Added simplification rules: hypot(X-Y*cos(Z), Y*sin(Z)) → Y*cosrule(X/Y, Z) and sin(π*x) → sinpi(x)

node_savings.eq37_cosine_rule: -4 nodes (hypot with trig → single cosrule call)

details.key_insight: cosrule(x,y) normalizes one side to 1, so the law of cosines sqrt(a²+b²-2ab*cos(θ)) becomes b*cosrule(a/b, θ), eliminating duplicate variable references. sinpi absorbs the π constant that appears in wave equations, saving 2 nodes per occurrence.
